Scenic drives home 🏔

North Wales (Llandudno / Conwy) → Cannock — three ways to swap the fast coast road for the mountains.

Baseline: the direct A55 coast road gets you home in ~1h45. The three routes below trade extra time for scenery — best if everyone's fresh and Alfie's likely to nap. Treat the sights as drive-throughs, not add-on visits, on a long beach day.
Route 1

The A5 Classic

Snowdonia villages & the Dee Valley

⏱ ~2h45🚗 ~115 mi+1h vs the A55

The best all-rounder: down the leafy Conwy Valley on the A470 to pretty Betws-y-Coed (Swallow Falls just beyond), then the classic Telford A5 through the hills to Llangollen on the River Dee and the sky-high Pontcysyllte Aqueduct, before Shrewsbury and home. Good cafe/leg-stretch points the whole way.

Via: Llandudno → Betws-y-Coed → Llangollen → Cannock

🗺️ Open this route in Google Maps
Route 2

The Mountain Epic

Ogwen Valley & the heart of Eryri

⏱ ~3h🚗 ~125 mi+1h15 vs the A55

The most dramatic option. Run west on the A55 to Bangor, then climb the A5 through Nant Ffrancon and the Ogwen Valley — the jagged peak of Tryfan rising straight out of Llyn Ogwen, with Snowdon's range all around. Drop through Capel Curig to Betws-y-Coed and pick up the A5 home. Slower mountain roads — a great one if Alfie naps.

Via: Llandudno → Bangor → Ogwen Valley → Betws-y-Coed → Cannock

🗺️ Open this route in Google Maps
Route 3

The Horseshoe Pass

A dramatic pass & the aqueduct — shorter

⏱ ~2h30🚗 ~110 mi+45 min vs the A55

Most scenery for the least detour. Head east off the coast to Ruthin, then over the sweeping Horseshoe Pass (A542) with its wide moorland views, dropping into Llangollen for the Pontcysyllte Aqueduct before the A5 home. Skips the western mountains but still feels like a proper Welsh mountain drive.

Via: Llandudno → Ruthin → Horseshoe Pass → Llangollen → Cannock
